claims.text: Wrong choices are easier and cheaper to execute than right choices; restructuring the choice architecture so that wrong choice becomes impossible eliminates both the execution and cost penalties.
claims.speaker: Unknown (speaker unattributed in fragment)
claims.mechanism: When choice architecture is altered so the wrong action is not an available option, the ease-of-wrong-choice penalty vanishes.

claims.text: Freedom of choice with massively constrained or limited option sets is operationally meaningless; such 'placebo choice' provides the illusion of autonomy without substantive decision-making power.
claims.speaker: Unknown (speaker unattributed in fragment)
conditions: options available do not represent genuine alternatives
bounded_by: Libertarian philosophy assumes meaningful freedom from choice abundance, which fails when option set is pre-filtered

examples.text: Restaurant menu offering still or sparkling water presents binary choice as autonomy when both options are pre-selected by the restaurant; customer perceives choice but has not selected the frame.
